description_long: This object is a detailed model of a World War II-era fighter airplane, specifically resembling a Supermarine Spitfire, displayed on a circular wooden base with textured grass terrain. The airplane features a streamlined fuselage with a single propeller at the nose, elliptical low-mounted wings, and a conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer. The aircraft is painted in a gray camouflage scheme with Royal Air Force roundels on the wings and fuselage, and the code 'FYOB' is visible on the side. The cockpit is enclosed with a clear canopy, positioned forward on the fuselage, and the landing gear is retracted, not visible in the display. The wooden base and grass terrain suggest this is a display or diorama piece, not a functional vehicle.
description: A detailed model of a WWII-era fighter airplane, painted in gray camouflage with RAF markings, displayed on a round wooden base with grass terrain.
description_view_0: Front view showing the propeller, elliptical wings, and nose details of the airplane.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the cockpit canopy, fuselage markings, and wing roundels.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail section, single vertical stabilizer, and rear fuselage.
description_view_3: Opposite angled side view showing the other side's markings and wing details.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, straight elliptical wings with no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single nose-mounted piston engine with a three-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Landing gear not visible; likely retracted or omitted for display.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed single-seat cockpit with a clear canopy.
